title: Mima
content:
Jemima is my name,
        But oh, I have another;
    My father always calls me Meg,
        And so do Bob and mother;
    Only my sister, jealous of
        The strands of my bright hair,
    'Jemima - Mima - Mima!'
        Calls, mocking, up the stair.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Walter De La Mare, titled "Mima", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
